economic_notes: BCN operates in Wabasca-Desmarais and communities of Calling Lake and Chipewyan Lake in northern Alberta. COVID-19 pandemic declared in March 2020 with closure of facilities, mandatory work from home, and receipt of additional grant funding. BCN guarantees loans for subsidiaries/partnerships totalling $14,705,000. Multiple related entities not consolidated including housing authorities, child and family services, health commission, education authority, and various trusts.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to missing budget information disclosures required under Canadian public sector accounting standards. Financial statements prepared on a NON-CONSOLIDATED basis - six related entities (Bigstone Business Trust, Bigstone Health Commission Business Trust, Bigstone Cree Nation Business Trust, Bigstone Economic Holdings, Bigstone Health Holdings, Bigstone Forestry Inc.) are accounted for at cost rather than consolidated. An additional 15 entities are not included in the financial statements at all. Management fees revenue dropped significantly from $8,960,000 in 2019 to $1,000,000 in 2020. ISC funding identified as INAC $36,083,330; Indigenous Services Canada (Health) $3,622,000 also from federal government. OSR calculated as total revenue $54,785,406 minus net recognized government transfers $41,503,838 = $13,281,568; however using gross government transfers of $45,537,773, OSR = $9,247,633. COVID-19 pandemic noted as subsequent event impacting operations.
